title: vicia andicola var. mathewsii
intro: Vicia andicola var. mathewsii, also known as the Andean vetch, is a type of legume that belongs to the genus Vicia and is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Vicia andicola var. mathewsii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems.
